sourceMovie.title: Cape Fear
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Cape Fear (1991) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Chinatown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cape Fear clicked for you, Chinatown (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cape Fear?
faq.answer: Try Chinatown, The Last House on the Left, The Brave One—each shares DNA with Cape Fear while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cape Fear?
faq.answer: Cape Fear (1991) is best described as Drama, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cape Fear worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
